title: Position AI Cockpit as collaborative environment design

sources.reason: Primary English positioning and top-level product narrative.
Chinese positioning must remain aligned with the English direction.
Japanese positioning must remain aligned with the English direction.
Design-principle document where collaborative environment design and 4D mapping will be explained.
Launch copy currently presents the older single-axis positioning and must be kept directionally consistent.

riskAssessment.reason: This task changes cross-document positioning language. The main risks are weakening the existing governance framing, introducing agent-specific wording, or letting translated docs drift from the English source direction.

executionDecision.reason: The requested scope is documentation-only, the boundaries are explicit, and the task can be implemented and verified through repository governance checks plus documentation quality validation.

preReviewWarnings: Review wording changes for accidental replacement of the existing AI Change Governance positioning instead of elevating it under a collaborative environment design frame.
Check multilingual docs and agent rule files for consistent treatment of unknowns, notCodable states, summaries, and checkpoints.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README and design philosophy describe AI Cockpit as collaborative environment design without removing AI Change Governance positioning.
4D Framework is documented as an explanatory operating model mapped to existing Cockpit mechanisms, not as new runtime schema.
Agent operating rules are updated consistently across generic, Claude, Gemini, and installed template rules.
No runtime scripts, guard policies, checks, CI, or tests are changed.
Chinese and Japanese documentation remain directionally aligned with the English positioning.
guidelines: Keep AI Change Governance as the core enforcement mechanism; add collaborative environment design above it rather than replacing it.
Treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as an explanatory operating model only; do not introduce them as required runtime schema fields.
Do not make the repository sound Claude-only or tied to any single agent vendor; wording must apply equally to Codex, Claude, Gemini, Cursor, Antigravity, and similar agents.
Preserve the existing bounded, reviewable, and auditable framing.
Do not change scripts, guard policies, checks, CI, or tests in this Work Item.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly requested coordinated documentation and agent-rule updates under .ai/** and top-level operating rule files.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
